roadmap.goal: Partition items inside each section card by aisle with dividers, aisle badges, and per-aisle progress so Carlos can confirm aisle boundaries and aisle-level completion mid-shop.

phases.name: Slice 01 — Aisle Partition + Dividers + Badges (US-01)
phases.description: Pure-function partition helper plus AisleSection render branch that draws dividers and aisle badges between aisle sub-groups. All-null and single-aisle sections must collapse to today's flat render path unchanged.

steps.name: Domain unit — slice 01 partition shape & ordering
steps.description: Introduce AisleKey and AisleSubGroup types and implement partitionSectionByAisle as a pure function. Returns null for collapse cases (single distinct aisle key, including all-null). Numeric keys ascending, then null-keyed tail. Counts derived from items in each bucket.
steps.criteria: Multi-aisle section card renders one sub-group per distinct `aisleNumber`, ascending. All-null section card renders no sub-groups and no aisle badge. Single-aisle section card renders no sub-group and no aisle badge. Mixed null + numeric section places the null sub-group at the tail with badge `No aisle`. Item input order is preserved inside each aisle sub-group (existing tie-break behaviour).
steps.scenario_name: Multi-aisle section partitions into ascending aisle sub-groups, All-null section collapses to flat, Single-aisle section collapses to flat, Mixed numeric + null section places null group at the tail, Item input order preserved inside each aisle sub-group
scenarios: Multi-aisle section partitions into ascending aisle sub-groups
Mixed numeric + null section places null group at the tail
Item input order preserved inside each aisle sub-group
acceptance_criteria: Multi-aisle section card renders one sub-group per distinct `aisleNumber`, ascending.
All-null section card renders no sub-groups and no aisle badge.
Single-aisle section card renders no sub-group and no aisle badge.
Mixed null + numeric section places the null sub-group at the tail with badge `No aisle`.
Item input order is preserved inside each aisle sub-group (existing tie-break behaviour).
steps.implementation_notes: Add `AisleKey = number | null` and `AisleSubGroup = { aisleKey, items, totalCount, checkedCount }` types to `src/domain/item-grouping.ts`. Implement `partitionSectionByAisle(group: SectionGroup): AisleSubGroup[] | null`. `null` return signals the collapse cases (single-aisle, all-null, 0 distinct keys) — caller falls back to the existing flat render path. Numeric keys ascending, then null-keyed tail. Each sub-group's `totalCount`/`checkedCount` derived from the items partitioned into that bucket. Pure function — no React, no fixtures, no I/O. Build domain test fixtures by feeding raw `TripItem`s through `groupBySection` (per DISTILL D4). The `it.skip` discipline is between slices: slice 02 domain tests stay skipped until slice 02 step.

steps.name: Component — slice 01 render branches (dividers + badges)
steps.description: Extend AisleSection to call partitionSectionByAisle and branch: null returns the existing flat render unchanged; non-null renders inline aisle sub-group blocks with divider + numeric badge (or `No aisle` badge for the null tail). Section header text and section-level X of Y / ✓ stay byte-identical on both branches.
steps.criteria: Each sub-group is preceded by a divider with a numeric aisle badge. All-null section card renders no sub-groups and no aisle badge. Single-aisle section card renders no sub-group and no aisle badge. Mixed null + numeric section places the null sub-group at the tail with badge `No aisle`.
steps.scenario_name: Multi-aisle section renders divider + badge per aisle group, All-null section renders flat — no sub-group nodes, Single-aisle section renders flat — no badge, Mixed section places `No aisle` tail at the end, Section header is unchanged on the sub-grouped branch (@noregress)
scenarios: Multi-aisle section renders divider + badge per aisle group
All-null section renders flat — no sub-group nodes
Single-aisle section renders flat — no badge
Mixed section places `No aisle` tail at the end
Section header is unchanged on the sub-grouped branch (D-NOREGRESS)
acceptance_criteria: Each sub-group is preceded by a divider with a numeric aisle badge.
All-null section card renders no sub-groups and no aisle badge.
Single-aisle section card renders no sub-group and no aisle badge.
Mixed null + numeric section places the null sub-group at the tail with badge `No aisle`.
steps.implementation_notes: Extend `src/ui/AisleSection.tsx` to call `partitionSectionByAisle(group)`. If it returns `null`, render the existing flat path unchanged (no behaviour change, regression-safe). If it returns sub-groups, render them inline as divider + badge + items blocks within the same section card — no new top-level component file. TestIDs per DESIGN D5: `aisle-subgroup-{aisleKey | 'no-aisle'}` for each sub-group node. The null tail uses badge label `No aisle`. Existing testIDs (`aisle-section-{section}`, `section-complete-{section}`) preserved. Use `@testing-library/react-native` `render` + `getByTestId` / `queryByTestId`, mirroring `StoreView.test.tsx` style. Build props by running `groupBySection` over `makeItem(...)` factories per DISTILL D4. Slice 02 component scenarios remain `it.skip` until 02-02.

phases.name: Slice 02 — Per-Aisle Progress + Completion Checkmark (US-02)
phases.description: Each aisle sub-group reports its own `checkedCount of totalCount` and a ✓ when complete. Section-level header (text, X of Y, ✓) remains byte-identical — verified by four @noregress scenarios.

steps.name: Domain unit — slice 02 per-aisle counts
steps.description: Verify `partitionSectionByAisle` reports per-aisle `checkedCount` / `totalCount` derived from the items partitioned into each bucket, including the `No aisle` tail.
steps.criteria: Each aisle sub-group renders `checkedCount of totalCount` for items within that aisle only. `No aisle` tail group in a mixed section also reports its own progress + checkmark.
steps.scenario_name: Each aisle sub-group reports its own checked / total counts, `No aisle` tail group reports its own counts
scenarios: Each aisle sub-group reports its own checked / total counts
`No aisle` tail group reports its own counts
acceptance_criteria: Each aisle sub-group renders `checkedCount of totalCount` for items within that aisle only.
`No aisle` tail group in a mixed section also reports its own progress + checkmark.
steps.implementation_notes: Unskip the slice 02 domain tests in `src/domain/item-grouping.test.ts`. PREPARE step: confirm whether 01-01 already wired `checkedCount` / `totalCount` correctly; if so, these tests should pass with no production change (the helper is already complete). If 01-01 left counts at 0/0 stubs, complete the count derivation in `partitionSectionByAisle` here. No new types or signatures — extends the same module. Counts always derived directly from items in each bucket; never trust upstream aggregates.

steps.name: Component — slice 02 progress + ✓ + noregress
steps.description: Render per-aisle `${checkedCount} of ${totalCount}` text and a ✓ when `checkedCount === totalCount` inside each sub-group block. Four @noregress scenarios assert the section-level header and section-level X of Y / ✓ stay byte-identical against today's behaviour on both flat and sub-grouped branches.
steps.criteria: Each aisle sub-group renders `checkedCount of totalCount` for items within that aisle only. Each aisle sub-group renders `✓` when `checkedCount === totalCount`. Section-level `X of Y` and `✓` continue to reflect the section as a whole (no behaviour change). All-null and single-aisle sections render no sub-group progress (only section progress). `No aisle` tail group in a mixed section also reports its own progress + checkmark.
steps.scenario_name: Aisle sub-group displays its own progress text, Aisle sub-group shows completion checkmark when fully checked, Section-level checkmark unaffected by partial-aisle completion (@noregress), Section-level checkmark shown when all aisles complete (@noregress), All-null section shows no sub-group progress (@noregress), Single-aisle section shows no sub-group progress (@noregress), `No aisle` tail in mixed section reports its own progress and ✓
scenarios: Aisle sub-group displays its own progress text
Aisle sub-group shows completion checkmark when fully checked
Section-level checkmark unaffected by partial-aisle completion (@noregress)
Section-level checkmark shown when all aisles complete (@noregress)
All-null section shows no sub-group progress (@noregress)
Single-aisle section shows no sub-group progress (@noregress)
`No aisle` tail in mixed section reports its own progress and ✓
acceptance_criteria: Each aisle sub-group renders `checkedCount of totalCount` for items within that aisle only.
Each aisle sub-group renders `✓` when `checkedCount === totalCount`.
Section-level `X of Y` and `✓` continue to reflect the section as a whole (no behaviour change).
All-null and single-aisle sections render no sub-group progress (only section progress).
`No aisle` tail group in a mixed section also reports its own progress + checkmark.
steps.implementation_notes: Extend the sub-group render block in `src/ui/AisleSection.tsx` to display `${checkedCount} of ${totalCount}` and a ✓ when complete. Add testID `aisle-subgroup-complete-{aisleKey | 'no-aisle'}` on the per-aisle ✓ node (per DESIGN D5). The section-level header (section name text, section-level `X of Y`, section-level ✓) MUST stay byte-identical on the sub-grouped branch — the four @noregress scenarios pin this contract. All-null and single-aisle render paths still go through the flat branch unchanged (collapse signal from `partitionSectionByAisle === null`). `StoreView.test.tsx` regression gate must remain green at every test run. No new files; no top-level subgroup component.

global_quality_gates: src/ui/StoreView.test.tsx remains green throughout (D-NOREGRESS regression gate)
No production code outside src/domain/item-grouping.ts and src/ui/AisleSection.tsx
No new test files outside src/domain/item-grouping.test.ts and src/ui/AisleSection.test.tsx
handoff_notes: Outside-in TDD with strict one-at-a-time discipline (DISTILL D7). Slice 02 scenarios (domain + component) start as `it.skip` and are unskipped only when their step begins. Within a step's scenarios, component-level tests may run together once GREEN. Domain tests are pure-function; no mocks, no fixtures (DISTILL CM-D). All aisle-grouping rules live in `partitionSectionByAisle` (ADR-005).
